list.title: Agents
list.subtitle: User-created reviewers. Each = provider + model + system prompt + linked skills.
list.emptyBody: An agent is a configured reviewer — a model, a prompt, and the skills it uses. Create one to start reviewing.

create.title: Create agent
create.subtitle: An agent is a configured reviewer — a model, a prompt, and the skills it uses.
create.defaultSystemPrompt: You are a code reviewer. Examine the diff and return at most 5 high-value findings, each citing an exact file:line.

editor.loadErrorBody: The agent could not be loaded.

config.title: Configuration
config.name: Name
config.modelHint: Models are loaded dynamically from the provider's /models.
config.modelEmptyHint: No models loaded — set the {provider} API key in Settings → API Keys.
config.systemPromptHint: Loaded as the static system message. Enabled skills are appended below it.
config.strategyHint: How the diff is sent to the model. Single pass = whole diff in one call (recommended); map-reduce = one call per file (for very large PRs); auto = single pass, switching to map-reduce on large diffs.

strategyOptions.single-pass: Single pass — whole diff in one call
strategyOptions.auto: Auto — single pass, map-reduce when large

config.ciFailOnHint: In GitHub Actions only: when should this agent's review block the PR (request changes + fail the check) vs just comment? Decided by finding severity, not the model. No effect on reviews shown in the studio.
config.repoIntelHint: Inject repo context (repo skeleton, callers of changed symbols, and a 'high blast-radius' note) into this agent's review prompt. On by default. Turning it off sends only the diff — useful for cheap models or self-contained PRs.

skills.title: Skills
skills.orderHint: Order matters — earlier skills appear earlier in the assembled prompt. Drag to reorder.
skills.empty: No skills in this workspace yet. Add one in the Skills Lab.

mount.body: Mount point for {owner}. The Agent Editor shell + tab routing are wired by A2 — {owner} renders this tab.
